content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Submit a Change of Address Online | California DMV

  - url: https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/online-change-of-address-coa-system/

  - source: California Department of Motor Vehicles

  - domain: dmv.ca.gov


  ## Content


  # Change of Address


  Complete this step before applying for or renewing other DMV products and services.
  Allow up to 3 days for processing. Check the status of your request by logging into
  your MyDMV account.


  Start address change.


  Important: To change the address of your Disabled Person Parking Placard (DPPP),
  use the separate DPPP Change of Address application.


  ## Before you begin


  1. A change of address (COA) does not require an in-person visit to a DMV office.


  2. Have both your old and new addresses handy.


  3. If you are changing the address for a vehicle, you will need to add it to your
  MyDMV garage.


  ## Are you eligible to change your address online?


  Some records cannot use the online Change of Address system. If any of the listed
  situations applies, complete a Change of Address (DMV 14) form and mail it to the
  address listed on the form.


  You may need to use the mailed DMV 14 form instead of the online system if you do
  not currently have a California DL/ID card, do not have a Social Security number,
  have a California commercial driver''s license and your residence address is in
  another state, have an Army Post Office (APO) or Fleet Post Office (FPO) address,
  have an address outside the United States, have applied for but have not received
  your REAL ID DL/ID card, or want to change the address on a Disabled Person Parking
  Placard.


  ## Frequently asked questions


  A residential address is where you physically reside. A mailing address is where
  you prefer to receive mail. If your change of address includes your DL/ID card,
  you must provide a residence address. You may use a PO box for your mailing address,
  but temporary mailing addresses are not allowed.


  New documents are not issued when you change your address. However, after you confirm
  that your address was changed successfully, you can request a replacement DL/ID
  or replacement registration card.


  DMV will not automatically update your address across documents when you only update
  your address for one. However, MyDMV gives you the option to change both your DL/ID
  card and vehicle/vessel registration at the same time if desired.


  You may only change your own address. You cannot change the address for someone
  else. You can change the address for up to three of your own vehicles in one transaction
  if they have the same address.


  A separate system handles Disabled Person Parking Placard change of address. Occupational
  license address changes are handled through DMV''s Occupational Licensing online
  system.


  You can use the Change of Address system if your new address is in another state,
  but you should check with the new state regarding driver license or vehicle registration
  requirements. A California CDL holder whose residence address is in another state
  cannot use the online change of address system because California residency is required
  to maintain a California CDL.


  Certain records require special handling. If you receive a message that you cannot
  submit the change of address online, complete a DMV 14 form and mail it to the address
  listed on the form.


  After 72 hours, log in to your MyDMV account and select "Check change of address
  status" for your driver''s license, ID card, or vehicle.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Submit a Change of Address Online | California DMV
url: https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/online-change-of-address-coa-system/

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Replacement Driver''s License Request | California DMV

  - url: https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/driver-licenses-identification-cards/replace-your-driver-license-or-identification-dl-id-card/online-duplicate-driver-license-request/

  - source: California Department of Motor Vehicles

  - domain: dmv.ca.gov


  ## Content


  # Replacement Driver''s License Request


  If your driver''s license (DL) has been lost, stolen, damaged, mutilated, or had
  a change of address, a replacement DL can be requested using this system.


  Start replacement DL request.


  ## Before you begin


  1. Log in to your MyDMV account, or create one if you have not already.


  2. If you recently submitted a Change of Address request, allow 3 days for processing
  before requesting a replacement DL. Your current address can be viewed in your MyDMV
  account.


  3. Be prepared to pay a fee for your replacement DL.


  ## What to expect


  Review current renewal processing times for an estimate of how long it will take
  for your new DL/ID card to arrive in the mail.


  If you have not received your DL card after 60 days, visit Driver''s License & ID
  Card Status.


  This online replacement request is for a replacement driver license. The broader
  replacement page explains that if a driver''s license (DL) or identification (ID)
  card has been lost, stolen, or damaged, a replacement may be requested. It also
  separates online replacement DL requests from replacement commercial driver license
  or ID card requests.


  For a replacement DL online, complete the Replacement Driver''s License application
  online. ID cards and commercial driver''s licenses (CDL) are not eligible for this
  specific application.


  For a replacement CDL or ID in an office, complete a new application online and
  then visit a DMV office to complete the process. A replacement CDL or ID request
  is not complete until the office visit is finished. Customers may make an appointment
  or check the current walk-in wait time for a local office. Be prepared to pay the
  appropriate replacement licensing fee, have a photograph validated by DMV, and scan
  a thumbprint to protect against identity theft.


  Replacement cards are mailed after processing. DMV states that a new card should
  generally arrive by mail within several weeks. If the replacement card does not
  arrive after 60 days, check DL/ID card status.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Replacement Driver's License Request | California DMV
url: https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/driver-licenses-identification-cards/replace-your-driver-license-or-identification-dl-id-card/online-duplicate-driver-license-request/

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Driver''s License or ID Card Updates | California DMV

  - url: https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/driver-licenses-identification-cards/updating-information-on-your-driver-license-or-identification-dl-id-card/

  - source: California Department of Motor Vehicles

  - domain: dmv.ca.gov


  ## Content


  # Driver''s License or ID Card Updates


  Change is inevitable, which is why DMV provides instructions for making sure a driver''s
  license or identification (DL/ID) card accurately reflects who and where you are.


  ## How to change your information


  ### Changing your name


  First, change your name with the Social Security Administration (SSA). When you
  apply for a new DL/ID card using your new name, DMV verifies your information with
  SSA. If the SSA information does not match the new DL/ID application information,
  the application will be denied and DMV will send a Request for Verification of Information
  letter.


  Next, complete a new DL/ID application. Use the online application to apply for
  a new DL/ID card or commercial driver''s license (CDL) with your new name. If you
  want the new DL/ID to be a REAL ID, you can apply for one in the application.


  Gather the required proof documents to show DMV. For a REAL ID as the updated DL/ID,
  provide proof of identity, Social Security Number, and two proofs of California
  residency. For a non-REAL ID DL/ID, provide proof of true full name. If applying
  for a CDL, additional commercial driver documents may be required.


  A name change is not complete until you come into a field office. Bring your current
  DL/ID and proof documents, be prepared to pay the appropriate licensing fee, and
  scan your thumbprint to protect against identity theft. If you applied for a DL,
  DMV will issue a temporary license valid for 60 days. A new DL/ID card is mailed
  within several weeks.


  ### Changing your address


  Notifying DMV of an address change is easy and free. Submit a Change of Address
  (DMV 14) form using the option most convenient for you.


  Online: request a change of address in your MyDMV account.


  By mail: download the DMV 14 form, or call DMV at 1-800-777-0133 to request a form
  by mail, complete it, and mail it to the address listed on the form.


  In person: visit any DMV office, complete a DMV 14, and give it to a representative.
  The representative will submit the form to DMV headquarters for processing.


  California law requires that you notify DMV within 10 days of changing your address.


  ### Changing your gender identity


  Individuals may self-select a gender identity of male (M), female (F), or nonbinary
  (X) to appear on a DL/ID. If you are changing your name in addition to gender category,
  follow the name-change instructions.


  Complete a new DL/ID application. If you want the updated DL/ID to be a REAL ID,
  you can apply for one in the application.


  If the updated DL/ID will be a REAL ID, gather proof of identity, Social Security
  Number, and two proofs of California residency. A gender category change is not
  complete until you come into a field office. Bring your current DL/ID and required
  proof documents, be prepared to pay the appropriate licensing fee, and scan your
  thumbprint.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Driver's License or ID Card Updates | California DMV
url: https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/driver-licenses-identification-cards/updating-information-on-your-driver-license-or-identification-dl-id-card/

Because this is an **in-state California move limited to an address change**, you can complete it online—no DMV office visit is required.

### Next steps

1. **Submit the address change through MyDMV**
   - [California DMV Change of Address](https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/online-change-of-address-coa-system/)
   - Have your old and new addresses ready.
   - California requires notification within **10 days of moving**.
   - The change is free and may take up to **3 days** to process.

2. **Verify the change after 72 hours**
   - Sign in to MyDMV and select **“Check change of address status.”**

3. **Request a replacement physical driver license**
   Your record indicates that you need a card showing the new address. After the address change is confirmed, wait the required 3 days, then use:
   - [Replacement Driver’s License Request](https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/driver-licenses-identification-cards/replace-your-driver-license-or-identification-dl-id-card/online-duplicate-driver-license-request/)
   - You’ll need to sign in to MyDMV and pay the replacement-license fee.

The DMV will mail the replacement card. Your current license remains active while the replacement is processed.

2. **Verify the change after 72 hours**

The DMV will mail the replacement card. Your current license remains active while the replacement is processed.
